svg.title: Spreadsheet-Native Intake

g.text: Direct Intake stays the dominant draft table, while PO Receiving remains a visible linked branch with separate ownership.
Spreadsheet rows prepare the intake payload; Direct Intake posts through inventory.intake, while PO Receiving remains a linked purchase-order receipt branch.

svg.text: The P0 Direct Intake columns fit in the main sheet by default, while PO Receiving remains a separate linked branch and deeper row detail stays in the inspector.
Bulk fill, duplicate, delete, and paste stay in-grid. Failed rows remain visible after submit attempts.

Support regions stay visibly adjacent: validation tells the operator what to fix, media stays linked to the selected row, and PO Receiving remains a visible alternative branch instead of an invisible implementation detail.
Upload, import, row-level evidence, and branch clarity remain directly under the main table so operators do not lose draft context while resolving blockers.
Use PO Receiving for purchase-order receipts

g.text: Selected row 3 · Cmd/Ctrl+S Save draft · Shift+Enter fill down · Cmd/Ctrl+R open PO receiving
Submit stays explicit. Direct Intake and PO Receiving remain visibly separate.
